perturbation.original_text: I acknowledge that The Business Partnership has provided, and/or has agreed to provide in the future, to me information of a confidential or proprietary nature (the Confidential Information) Confidential Information shall mean any information or data relating to any clients of The Business Partnership business or affairs disclosed whether in writing, orally or by any other means.
perturbation.changed_text: I acknowledge that The Business Partnership has provided, and/or has agreed to provide in the future, to me information of a confidential or proprietary nature (the Confidential Information) Confidential Information shall mean only written documents or data explicitly labeled confidential and directly concerning the financial performance of clients of The Business Partnership.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' is broad, encompassing any information relating to clients. The revised definition narrows it to explicitly labeled financial performance data presented in written form. This creates a contradiction: under the original definition, oral disclosures are confidential; under the revised definition, they are not. This affects the scope of protection afforded to information disclosed by The Business Partnership and introduces uncertainty as to what constitutes 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I acknowledge that The Business Partnership has provided, and/or has agreed to provide in the future, to me information of a confidential or proprietary nature (the Confidential Information) Confidential Information shall mean only written documents or data explicitly labeled confidential and directly concerning the financial performance of clients of The Business Partnership business or affairs disclosed whether in writing, orally or by any other means. I agree that in respect of the Confidential Information received from The Business Partnership I will: Treat it as confidential and make no copies thereof not disclose it to any third party without the prior written consent of The Business Partnership and use it solely for the purpose as agreed between parties
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only written documents or data explicitly labeled confidential and directly concerning the financial performance of clients. The contradicted text initially uses the same definition as the original text and then states 'business or affairs disclosed whether in writing, orally or by any other means', which directly contradicts the new, narrower definition in the changed text that excludes oral disclosures and broadens to any information 'relating to any clients of The Business Partnership business or affairs'.

perturbation.original_text: I acknowledge that this Agreement shall remain in force in perpetuity.
perturbation.changed_text: I acknowledge that this Agreement shall remain in force for a period of one year from the date of signing.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement is stated to be 'in force in perpetuity', meaning forever. The changed text limits the agreement to one year. This is a fundamental contradiction concerning the agreement's duration, directly impacting its enforceability after the initial year. It is unclear which term holds.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I acknowledge that this Agreement shall remain in force for a period of one year from the date of signing. The laws of England and Wales shall govern this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement is in force 'in perpetuity', while the changed text limits it to 'one year'. The contradicted text re-states the 'one year' duration. The change from perpetual to one year creates a direct contradiction regarding the agreement's duration. The change results in a misalignment and therefore an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All recipients the Sales Particulars and any other details irrevocably agree:- 1. To treat all information contained herein, and as may subsequently be supplied, with complete confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: All recipients of the Sales Particulars and any other details agree to treat only the financial details with complete confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, 'all information' was to be treated with confidentiality. The changed text restricts this to 'financial details'. This introduces a contradiction as to what information is considered confidential under the terms and conditions. Sales Particulars are no longer subject of confidentiallity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All recipients of the Sales Particulars and any other details agree to treat only the financial details with complete confidentiality:- 1. To treat all information contained herein, and as may subsequently be supplied, with complete confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'all information' is to be treated with complete confidentiality. The changed text narrows this to 'financial details' only. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, as the contradicted text states 'all information...with complete confidentiality' which includes Sales Particulars. The changed text now excludes the Sales Particulars from the confidentiality agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: To make no approach directly to the Owner or the Business and or Property either personally, by telephone, or in writing without the consent of TBP.
perturbation.changed_text: To make no approach directly to the Owner of the Business either personally, by telephone, or in writing without the consent of TBP. Approaching the Property is permitted
perturbation.explanation: The original clause prevents any contact with the Owner, Business, or Property. The change now permits approaching the Property, creating a contradiction. This can lead to disputes over what constitutes an 'approach' to the Business and Property.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. To make no approach directly to the Owner of the Business either personally, by telephone, or in writing without the consent of TBP. Approaching the Property is permitted 3. To visit the Owner and/or the Business or Property only and strictly by prior appointment to be made and confirmed through the Office of TBP.
perturbation.justification: The original text forbids any approach to the Owner, Business, or Property without consent. The changed text now explicitly permits approaching the Property. The contradicted text contains both of these statements, which creates a direct contradiction within the document. One part of the contradicted text says approaching the property is allowed, and the other states that any visit to the Property requires prior appointment and confirmation through the office of TBP. This adheres to the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: That in the event of agreeing to purchase the Business and/or Property will pay to TBP a deposit as a sign of good faith amounting to 5% of the purchase price but subject to a minimum of £5,000. This deposit will be held by TBP in a Non-Interest Earning Client Account, and will be applied to the purchase price at the time of completion. This deposit will be paid "Subject to Contract" and will be refundable (subject to clauses 2 and 3 below).
perturbation.changed_text: That in the event of agreeing to purchase the Business will pay to TBP a deposit as a sign of good faith amounting to 5% of the purchase price but subject to a minimum of £5,000. This deposit will be held by TBP in a Non-Interest Earning Client Account, and will be applied to the purchase price at the time of completion. This deposit will be paid "Subject to Contract" and will be refundable (subject to clauses 2 and 3 below). However, the deposit is non-refundable if the purchase concerns Property.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the deposit is refundable (subject to clauses 2 and 3). The changed text adds that the deposit is non-refundable if the purchase concerns Property, creating a direct contradiction. This makes the terms regarding the deposit ambiguous and introduces risk.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. That in the event of agreeing to purchase the Business will pay to TBP a deposit as a sign of good faith amounting to 5% of the purchase price but subject to a minimum of £5,000. This deposit will be held by TBP in a Non-Interest Earning Client Account, and will be applied to the purchase price at the time of completion. This deposit will be paid "Subject to Contract" and will be refundable (subject to clauses 2 and 3 below). However, the deposit is non-refundable if the purchase concerns Property.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the deposit is refundable (subject to clauses 2 and 3). The changed text adds that the deposit is non-refundable if the purchase concerns Property. This creates a direct and clear contradiction as the same deposit is stated to be refundable and non-refundable based on the same condition. This falls under Misaligned Terminology as a change in the conditions of the terminology creates an in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text is exactly the same as the Changed Text, which makes the contradiction clear as it is contradictory to itself.
